_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Wednesday March 11 | 3:06 p.m 

Bonjour Tremblant,

We’re preparing the mountain for our reopening tomorrow following today’s freezing rain. Our teams, including our grooming crews, are working around the clock to deliver the best possible conditions.

Please note that the Soleil and Edge chairlifts will be closed.
Residents on the Soleil side of the mountain are invited to make their way to the South side to begin their day.

First Tracks has been cancelled.

Terrain parks will open gradually as conditions allow, and several runs will open progressively throughout the day

We invite you to check this page regularly; it will be updated if there are any significant changes. 

Thank you for your understanding, and we look forward to welcoming everyone back on the mountain tomorrow.

Mountain safety tip of the week: Remove your backpack.
In chairlifts, to ensure you are seated properly, remove your backpack and place it on your lap or, if space allows, on the seat. This reduces the risk of accidents or of straps getting caught in the lift.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
